strengths: Successfully completed the circle cutting task with proper traction setup
Maintained consistent instrument control throughout the cutting phases
Achieved reasonable completion time of 137.5 seconds for intermediate level
weaknesses: Two visible line deviations from the marked circle during cutting phases
Inconsistent precision when following the circular path
Lost accuracy during sustained cutting motions

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between each quarter to reset positioning and verify alignment with the marked line.
drills.targets_weakness: Line deviations from the marked circle during cutting phases.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: Practice 15 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland forceps to improve scissor entry angle and maintain optimal cutting geometry throughout the circle.
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent precision when following the circular path.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent completion ability into more precise line-following technique. Work on maintaining cutting accuracy throughout sustained motions to reduce penalty deductions.
